page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.large is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate entry-level capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1303.98, while the multi-core score reaches 1735.19. Memory bandwidth tests show 95.52 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 12.93 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 11.31 GB/sec for large-block writes. PassMark CPU and memory scores are 2120.23 and 1374.19 respectively, reflecting a modest performance profile under heavy computational loads.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.c6.large offers a predictable but lightweight compute profile. It is suitable for low-traffic static web serving, which benchmarked at 1.93 GB/sec, basic Redis caching, and lightweight microservices. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, making it a cost-efficient choice for distributed, stateless application tiers where high memory density is unnecessary.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.large is an entry-level, compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based server provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It contains no local storage or GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network connection. Benchmark results show modest performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1303.98 and a multi-core score of 1735.19. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 12.93 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. This instance is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, small Redis deployments, and stateless microservices.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance with a 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio, designed for lightweight stateless workloads and basic web serving.
